cross_scene_arcs.title: From Detachment to Desire: Frank's Emotional Awakening
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Frank evolves from emotional isolation and disengagement into active pursuit of intimacy with Cara, marking a profound shift from passive observer to emotionally invested participant.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Frank seeks solitude and emotional distance, absorbed in his book and avoiding connection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Frank actively seeks out Cara in Venice, expressing his desire to spend time with her and forming a mutual connection.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The sustainability of Frank's emotional openness and whether this connection is genuine or situational remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Illusion of Partnership: Frank's Role in the Con
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Frank transitions from an unwitting bystander to a fully integrated participant in Cara's con, assuming the role of her husband and gaining access to privileged spaces, though his internal expectations clash with the reality of their arrangement.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Frank is publicly acknowledged as Cara's spouse, entering the con under false pretenses and with visible discomfort.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Frank embraces the role fully, choosing intimacy over propriety by joining Cara on the balcony and becoming physically involved.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The mismatch between Frank's emotional investment and the likely transactional nature of the con creates tension about his true status and value in the operation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Truth, Identity, and Betrayal: The Unraveling of Trust
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After discovering the engraving on Cara's watch, Frank's growing suspicion triggers a psychological and physical crisis, culminating in a violent escape that reveals the depth of the conspiracy he's entangled in.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Frank begins to assert emotional honesty and perceptiveness, confronting Cara about the watch and gaining her respect.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Frank is injured, hunted, and fleeing across Venice, realizing he is part of a dangerous scheme far beyond his understanding.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Frank does not know who Alexander Pearce is or why he is being mistaken for him, leaving his identity and safety in question.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rejection and Resilience: From Victim to Agent
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After being abandoned by Cara and nearly killed, Frank transforms from a confused, rejected man into a determined agent seeking truth and confrontation, reclaiming agency despite overwhelming danger.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Frank is left emotionally shattered and isolated after Cara abruptly ends their intimacy and locks herself away.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Frank boldly confronts the network hunting him, demanding to meet Alexander Pearce and asserting control over his own fate.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Frank's confrontation fails when he targets the wrong man, and he is soon captured, leaving his quest for truth unfulfilled.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Watch and the Shadow: Lingering Mysteries of Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The engraved watch becomes a symbol of hidden identity and manipulation, tying Frank to Alexander Pearce and driving his journey from emotional discovery to existential crisis and confrontation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Frank accepts the watch as a token of intimacy and trust, unaware of its deeper significance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Frank uses the watch as a key to uncover the conspiracy, declaring his intent to confront Pearce and reclaim his identity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The true identity and fate of Alexander Pearce remain unknown, and Frank's role in the larger scheme continues to blur the line between self and impersonation.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Bait and the Mind Game
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ackerman devises and executes a psychological strategy to capture Alexander Pearce by using Cara Mason as emotional bait, shifting from traditional investigative methods to behavioral manipulation. Despite operational disruptions—especially from Frank—Ackerman repeatedly reasserts control, adapting the plan to maintain leverage and momentum toward luring Pearce.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ackerman proposes using Cara Mason as bait based on Alexander's emotional attachments.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ackerman reestablishes operational focus by deploying surveillance around Pearce's villa after a disruption, maintaining the bait strategy.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether the bait strategy will succeed given Frank's interference and Pearce's unpredictability.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Authority and the Erosion of Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ackerman's authority is repeatedly challenged—first by Frank's unsanctioned actions, then by internal sabotage and external attacks—leading to a cascade of operational breaches. His responses grow increasingly aggressive, revealing a leader under pressure whose control is slipping despite efforts to dominate through force and psychological tactics.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Frank disrupts the operation, undermining Ackerman's control.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Surveillance systems are sabotaged and agents are killed, marking a near-total breakdown in operational security.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The true source of the sabotage and whether it stems from within the team or an external adversary remains unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Cara Mason: Asset, Obsession, and Loss
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ackerman's relationship with Cara Mason evolves from strategic asset to emotionally charged dependency. He reveals personal vulnerability in defending her role, justifies her endangerment for the mission, and ultimately suffers a profound professional and personal defeat when she is lost to Frank. The arc underscores his blurred lines between duty and emotional investment.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ackerman reveals his strategy to use Cara as bait, based on her emotional connection to Pearce.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ackerman loses Cara entirely, acknowledging he was outmaneuvered and that his emotional reliance on her compromised the mission.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The true nature of Ackerman's personal feelings for Cara and whether she was ever loyal to him remains ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Command to Isolation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ackerman begins as the undisputed leader of an international manhunt, but over time, his authority becomes increasingly contested and isolated. Internal dissent, personal bitterness, and strategic failures erode his standing, culminating in a partial victory that leaves him professionally marginalized and emotionally detached.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ackerman unifies international agencies and establishes himself as the central authority on the Pearce case.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite securing evidence and making an arrest, Ackerman faces professional isolation as Jones signals the investigation's likely end.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Ackerman will be held accountable for his methods or continue to operate in the shadows despite institutional disengagement.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Analog Mind vs. the Digital Void
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ackerman relies on psychological insight and analog tradecraft to track a digitally elusive adversary, but this strength becomes a vulnerability as real-world breaches—assassinations, sabotage, and infiltration—undermine his control. The arc blends intellectual strategy with operational collapse, highlighting the limits of mind games in the face of physical threats.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ackerman explains Pearce's use of analog communication, asserting strategic superiority through behavioral analysis.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Despite maintaining discipline in the face of sabotage, Ackerman's team is blind to key threats, and the mission hangs in the balance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether analog reasoning can prevail when the enemy operates both psychologically and lethally in the physical world.
